title: Allowing browsers to sniff MIME types is security-sensitive
description: Allowing browsers to sniff MIME types is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity Hotspotexpress.jsMIME confusionattacks occur when an attacker successfully tricks a web-browser to interpret a resource as a different type than the one expected. To correctly interpret a resource (script, image, stylesheet …​) web browsers look for theContent-Type headerdefined in the HTTP response received from the server, but often this header is not set or is set with an incorrect value. To avoid content-type mismatch and to provide the best user experience, web browsers try to deduce the right content-type, generally by inspecting the content of the resources (the first bytes).
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.
